json_data.summary: This Lab 1 Report for the WIA2004 Operating System course, prepared by Group 7 (tutorial group OCC 4) and its team members, focuses on CPU Scheduling Algorithms.

The report details the FCFS algorithm through sections on its introduction, flowchart, pseudocode, Java coding, and sample output, also providing pseudocode for time calculations and referencing an external video.

FCFS is a simple, non-preemptive CPU scheduling algorithm that follows the FIFO principle, allocating the CPU to the first arriving process, which then executes without interruption; it is easy to understand and implement and gives no priority to any process, executing simultaneously arriving processes based on their queue order.

While FCFS is easy to implement, it can lead to poor performance with long waiting and turnaround times, especially when shorter processes are delayed, though processes with a burst time of 0 complete immediately, resulting in equal turnaround and waiting times.
Turnaround Time is calculated as Completion Time minus Arrival Time, while Waiting Time is Turnaround Time minus Burst Time.
